summary: Operate the site-to-site VPN connections: add or remove static routes with guardrails, retag connections self-service, and route the engineer-reviewed work — tunnel PSK rotation, tunnel inside-CIDR changes (forces replacement), new connection provisioning, and decommissioning.

operations.title: Add a static route to a VPN connection
operations.summary: Add a route so another on-premises network can reach you through this VPN. An engineer finishes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Route another on-premises network through an existing static-routing VPN connection. The route becomes its own resource — the estate does not manage any yet, so an engineer adopts the first one from the generated draft, reviews and finishes it.

params.name: vpn_connection
params.label: VPN connection
params.help: Static-routing VPN connection that carries the new route.

params.name: destination_cidr
params.label: Destination CIDR
params.help: The on-premises network to reach through this VPN, like 10.2.0.0/16.

decisions: The estate's VPN file currently declares route resources out of scope — adopting the first one is part of this change

operations.title: Remove static route from VPN connection
operations.summary: Remove a static route from a VPN connection. Traffic to that network over this tunnel stops unless another path already carries it.
operations.description: Deletes an existing static route entry from a VPN connection; traffic to that CIDR over this tunnel will stop unless another path already carries it.

params.name: route
params.label: Static route
params.help: Existing static route entry to remove.

operations.title: Update VPN connection tags
operations.summary: Update tags on a VPN connection. Metadata only - tunnels and routing are unaffected.
operations.description: Adds or updates cost-allocation and ownership tags on the VPN connection; purely metadata, no effect on tunnels or routing.

params.name: vpn_connection
params.label: VPN connection
params.help: VPN connection whose tags are updated, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed connection can be selected; tags are metadata and never touch tunnels or routing.

params.name: tags
params.label: Tags
params.help: Key/value tags to merge onto the connection, e.g. Environment=prod, CostCentre=Network.

operations.title: Rotate VPN tunnel pre-shared key
operations.summary: Rotate a VPN tunnel's pre-shared key from a Secrets Manager secret. The tunnel drops and reconnects, causing a brief interruption.
operations.description: Points a tunnel's pre-shared key at a new secret held in Secrets Manager and triggers renegotiation; the affected tunnel drops and re-establishes during the swap, so expect a brief interruption on that path (the paired tunnel should carry traffic if the on-prem side is HA-configured).

params.name: vpn_connection
params.label: VPN connection
params.help: Site-to-site VPN connection whose tunnel pre-shared key is being rotated, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed connection can be targeted.

params.name: tunnel_number
params.label: Tunnel
params.help: Which of the two tunnels to rotate.

params.name: psk_secret_name
params.label: New PSK secret name
params.help: Name of the Secrets Manager secret holding the new pre-shared key. Never paste the raw key here — create the secret first, then reference it by name.

operations.title: Change VPN tunnel inside CIDR
operations.summary: Change a VPN tunnel's inside address block. Applying REPLACES the connection - both tunnels go down until it's rebuilt.
operations.description: Changes a tunnel's inside IPv4 /30 block; AWS requires the connection to be recreated for this, tearing down and re-establishing both tunnels and causing an outage on this VPN until it comes back up.

params.name: vpn_connection
params.label: VPN connection
params.help: Site-to-site VPN connection whose tunnel inside CIDR is changing, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed connection can be targeted for this replacement-level change.

params.name: tunnel_number
params.label: Tunnel
params.help: Which of the connection's tunnels gets the new inside CIDR; every AWS site-to-site VPN connection has exactly two tunnels, so 1 and 2 are the only valid choices.

params.name: new_inside_cidr
params.label: New tunnel inside CIDR

bounds.semantic: RFC 3927 /30 per AWS VPN tunnel requirements, not already in use by another tunnel

params.help: New link-local /30 for the tunnel's inside interface, e.g. 169.254.100.0/30.

operations.title: Provision new site-to-site VPN connection
operations.summary: Provision a new site-to-site VPN connection with two tunnels. An engineer coordinates keys and routing with on-prem before cutover.
operations.description: Creates a new IPSec VPN connection between an existing customer gateway and VPN gateway, provisioning two tunnels; requires coordinating pre-shared keys and static/BGP routing with the on-prem network team before cutover.

params.name: vpn_gateway
params.label: VPN gateway
params.help: Existing VPN gateway (VGW) the new connection will attach to.

params.name: customer_gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: Existing customer gateway representing the on-prem VPN device.

params.name: connection_name
params.label: Connection name
params.help: Short identifier for the new connection, e.g. dc2-tertiary-vpn.

params.name: static_routes_only
params.label: Static routing only
params.help: True for static routes; false to enable BGP dynamic routing over the tunnels.

operations.title: Decommission VPN connection
operations.summary: Permanently delete a VPN connection and both tunnels. Confirm nothing still depends on it first - this can't be undone.
operations.description: Permanently deletes a site-to-site VPN connection and both its tunnels, removing on-prem connectivity over this path; confirm no static routes, BGP sessions, or on-prem routing still depend on it before proceeding, as this cannot be undone.

params.name: vpn_connection
params.label: VPN connection
params.help: The VPN connection to permanently decommission, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed connection whose state is fully known to the repo can be deleted.

operations.title: Rename customer gateway device
operations.summary: Rename the on-premises device label. Applying REPLACES the gateway and its VPN connection, causing an outage until on-prem is reconfigured.
operations.description: Set device_name, the label for the customer gateway device. CAUTION: on aws_customer_gateway every argument except tags is ForceNew (EC2 has no modify API) — changing device_name REPLACES the customer gateway, and any aws_vpn_connection referencing it is replaced with it: new tunnel outside IPs and pre-shared keys, so the on-prem firewall must be reconfigured. Site-to-site VPN outage until that is done. Engineer-authored change only.

params.name: gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: Customer gateway whose device_name is changed, chosen from the estate inventory. Warning: this attribute is ForceNew — the gateway and its dependent VPN connection are replaced, which drops the tunnels until the on-prem side is reconfigured.

params.name: device_name
params.label: Device name
params.help: Friendly label for the on-prem device. Letters, numbers, spaces and . _ : / @ + = - up to 255 chars.

operations.downtime: Site-to-site VPN outage while the customer gateway and dependent VPN connection are replaced (new outside IPs + PSKs; on-prem reconfig required)
operations.terraformCapability: -/+ replace (device_name is ForceNew; cascades to aws_vpn_connection)

operations.title: Change customer gateway outside IP address
operations.summary: Change the IP the customer gateway points at. Applying REPLACES the gateway and rebuilds the VPN — tunnels drop until it's back.
operations.description: Update ip_address, the IPv4 outside-interface address of the customer gateway device. On aws_customer_gateway every argument except tags is ForceNew, so changing this replaces the customer gateway and the VPN connection referencing it; both tunnels drop until the VPN is rebuilt. Bounded to a valid IPv4.

params.name: gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: Customer gateway whose outside IP address is changing, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_customer_gateway can be repointed at a new on-prem peer.

params.name: ip_address
params.label: Outside IPv4 address
params.help: Public IPv4 of the on-prem VPN device's outside interface, e.g. 203.0.113.10. Tunnels will re-establish against this peer.

operations.title: Add a tag to customer gateway
operations.summary: Add a metadata tag to a customer gateway. No effect on tunnels.
operations.description: Add a single key/value entry to the customer gateway tags map. In-place update, cosmetic/metadata only. Reversible (remove the tag), no downtime.

params.name: gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: Customer gateway that receives the new tag, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_customer_gateway can be modified; tags are metadata and never affect tunnels.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key, up to 128 chars. Letters, numbers, spaces and . _ : / @ + = -

params.name: value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value, up to 256 chars.

operations.title: Remove a tag from customer gateway
operations.summary: Remove a metadata tag from a customer gateway. Reversible, and tunnels are unaffected.
operations.description: Remove a single key from the customer gateway tags map. In-place update, cosmetic/metadata only. Reversible (re-add the tag), no downtime.

params.name: gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: Customer gateway whose tag is removed, chosen from the estate inventory so only a Terraform-managed aws_customer_gateway can be modified; removing a tag never affects tunnels.

params.name: key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Existing tag key to remove, up to 128 chars.

operations.title: Request another change to customer gateway
operations.summary: Request any other change to a customer gateway, including ones that replace it. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all for any customer gateway change not offered as a bounded form — e.g. rotating certificate_arn, changing bgp_asn/bgp_asn_extended (ForceNew), or moving region. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: The customer gateway to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Provision a new VPN connection (draft resource)
operations.summary: Author a new site-to-site VPN connection as a complete draft; an engineer reviews it and coordinates on-prem details first.
operations.description: Stand up a new aws_vpn_connection between an existing VPN gateway and customer gateway. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review. Requires coordinating pre-shared keys and static/BGP routing with the on-prem network team before cutover. Same inputs as the existing guided provisioning action; this form authors the raw resource skeleton instead.

params.name: connection_key
params.label: Connection identifier
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the connection in the generated draft.

params.name: vpn_gateway
params.label: VPN gateway
params.help: Existing VPN gateway (VGW) the new connection attaches to.

params.name: customer_gateway
params.label: Customer gateway
params.help: Existing customer gateway representing the on-prem VPN device.

params.name: type
params.label: Connection type
params.help: The only connection type AWS offers — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: static_routes_only
params.label: Static routing only
params.help: True for static routes; false to enable BGP dynamic routing over the tunnels.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: VPN connection name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this connection is for
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this connection.

decisions: A site-to-site VPN connection extends the VPC's private network to on-premises — confirm the on-prem routing/firewall team is coordinated before cutover, and that this does not unintentionally expose internal ranges
Tunnel pre-shared keys, inside CIDRs and BGP details are left to AWS defaults if unset — set them afterward with the existing rotate/change actions if the on-prem side requires specific values
Static routes into this connection are a separate follow-up step (the existing add-a-static-route action)
